ps140: 4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

You enlightened your mind* with the spiritual radiance,* becoming a shining sun!* You extended the rays of your teachings* to all the ends of the earth,* enlightening the faithful,* driving away the darkness of heresies,* all-blessed Godbearer,** with the power of Him Who shone forth from the Virgin!

The whole Church is adorned,* O holy Cyril,* with the good words of your preaching,* and is arrayed with devotion* in fitting beauty.* She honours your holy and venerable memory* in a holy manner,* O boast of the orthodox, height of the fathers,** champion of the most holy Virgin at the council!

All the tinder of heresies* is burned with your fiery teachings,* O all-wise one!* The army of the impious* who would not submit to the truth* is drowned in the depths of your understanding,* O holy Cyril!* The Church of the faithful is ever adorned with your doctrines,** honouring you with mighty voices!

Good and faithful servant,* labourer in the vineyard of Christ,* you bore the burden of the day* and increased the talent given to you.* You did not envy those who came after you,* therefore the gates of heaven have been opened to you!* Enter into the joy of your Lord** and pray for us, Cyril our father!
stavrotheotokion: The unblemished ewe-lamb and immaculate Mistress,* when, of old, she beheld her Lamb upon the tree of the Cross,* exclaimed maternally and, marvelling, cried out:* O my Child most sweet, what new and most strange sight is this that I see?* How have the thankless synagogue betrayed You to the judgment-seat of Pilate* and condemned You to death, who are the Life of all?** Yet I sing of Your ineffable condescension, O Word!
aposticha: O man of God, faithful servant,* minister of the Lord, man of divine desires,* chosen vessel, pillar and ground of the Church,* heir to the Kingdom:** Never cease to cry out to the Lord for us!
stavrotheotokion_aposticha: Seeing You crucified, O Christ, she who gave You birth cried aloud:* "What is this strange mystery which I behold, O my Son?* How is it that You die, suspended in the flesh upon the Tree,* O Bestower of life?"
